new_classes.label_en: rum or other sugar-cane spirit in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.label_de: Rum oder anderer Zuckerrohrbranntwein in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirit obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (rum, taffia, and similar), retaining typical organoleptic characteristics of the product type, presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, as classified under CN subheading 2208 40 91.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) production by distillation of fermented sugar-cane products (linked via hasDistillationProcess → SugarCaneDistillationProcess and hasDistillationFeedstock → FermentedSugarCaneProduct); (2) retention of typical organoleptic/sensory characteristics (linked via RO:has_quality → OrganolepticCharacteristicsQuality); (3) container size > 2 L (linked via BFO:has_part → LargeContainer); (4) disjointness from sibling CN 2208 40 51 (containers ≤ 2 L) via complement restriction. A small gap remains: the legal note references specific HS Explanatory Notes (heading 2208, third paragraph, item 3) naming rum and taffia explicitly. This definitional provenance cannot be fully captured in OWL axioms without introducing named individuals for product types, so coverage is slightly reduced.
